series_description: Audits and reviews performed by or on behalf of the agency, including the working papers that support the audit. Also includes audits performed on the agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years
comments:
NOTE: The State Auditor's Office
retains any copies of its audits
performed on Texas state agencies.

series_description: Including any supporting documentation created and/or used to justify and support legislative appropriations requests by the agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version
of Legislative Appropriation
Requests must be submitted to the
Texas State Publications Depository
Program per 13 TAC 3.3(a)(3)(A).
Working files and related
documentation used in creating the
final plan are not subject to
archival review and may be
disposed of at the expiration of the
retention period.

series_description: Incoming/outgoing and internal correspondence pertaining to the formulation, planning, implementation, interpretation, modification, or redefinition of the programs, services, or projects of the agency and the administrative regulations, policies, and procedures that govern them.
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only the
administrative correspondence of
executive staff, board or
commission members, division
directors and program heads
require archival review. Contact
the State Archives when these
records have met their retention
periods. CAUTION: This records
series and item number 1.1.008
should be used only for
correspondence that is not
included in or directly related to
another records series on the
agency’s approved schedule. For
example, a memorandum that
documents an appropriations
request must be retained for the
minimum retention period
prescribed by item number 1.1.004;
a letter concerning an audit for that
prescribed by item number 1.1.002,
etc.

series_description: Non-administrative incoming/outgoing and internal correspondence, in any media, pertaining to or arising from the routine operations of the policies, programs, services, or projects of the agency.
comments:
SEE NOTE in item number 1.1.007.
SEE ALSO item number 1.1.010.

series_description: Any document that officially initiates, rescinds, or amends general office procedures.

Calendars, appointment books or programs and scheduling, or itinerary records, purchased with state funds or maintained by staff during business hours that document appointments, itineraries, and other activities of agency officials or employees.
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only the
calendars, appointment, and
itinerary records of elected
officials, executive staff, board or
commission members, division
directors, and program heads
require archival review. Contact
the State Archives when these
records have met their retention
periods. CAUTION: A record of this
type purchased with personal
funds, but used by a state official or
employee to document his or her
work activities may be a state
record and subject to this retention
period. See Open Records Decision
635 issued in December 1995 by
the Attorney General.

series_description: News, press releases, or any public relations files maintained or issued by the agency. Includes print, electronic, audio, and audiovisual records.
Includes all correspondence and documentation relating to requests for records that may be furnished to the public under Rule 12 of the Texas Rules of Judicial Information.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year
comments:
NOTE: The Judicial Branch is
governed by the Rules of Judicial
Administration Rule 12 in regard to
Public Access to Judicial Records.
The purpose of Rule 12 is to
provide public access to
information in the judiciary
consistent with the mandates of
the Texas Constitution that the
public interests are best served by
open courts and by an independent
judiciary.

series_description: Includes all correspondence and documentation relating to requests for records that are exempt under Rule 12 of the Texas Rules of Judicial Information or Tex. Gov’t Code §552.003(1)(B).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
NOTE: The Judicial Branch is
governed by the Rules of Judicial
Administration Rule 12 in regard to
Public Access to Judicial Records.
The purpose of Rule 12 is to
provide public access to
information in the judiciary
consistent with the mandates of
the Texas Constitution that the
public interests are best served by
open courts and by an independent
judiciary.

series_description: Charts or diagrams that show the structure of an agency and the relationships and relative ranks of its departments and job positions.
comments:
NOTE: Organization Charts are
included as supporting
documentation in the Court’s
Legislative Appropriation Requests
submitted to the Legislative Budget
Board. ARCHIVES NOTE: Only
charts showing the overall
arrangement and administrative
structure of the state agency need
to be transferred to the Archives
and Information Services Division,
Texas State Library and Archives
Commission. Organizational charts
showing division/department level
detail are not considered archival.

series_description: Notes or text of speeches, papers, presentations, or reports delivered in conjunction with agency work.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
NOTE: These materials shall be
owned and maintained by the
author/presenter of such or their
designee until such time as they
may be presented for review for
archival purposes. ARCHIVES NOTE:
Only speeches, papers, and
presentations given by or on behalf
of executive staff, board or
commission members, division
directors and program heads
require archival review.

series_description: Records created by or on behalf of the agency in anticipation of or in the adjudication of a lawsuit.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year
comments:
This item number is not in
reference to, nor is it applicable to,
any case on appeal pending or
otherwise before this Court.
ARCHIVES NOTE: Cases that set
legal precedent or exhibit historical
value will be evaluated by the
Archives and Information Services
Division of the Texas State Library
and Archives Commission for
archival preservation.

series_description: Self evaluations and plans documenting compliance with the requirements of the Americans with Disabilities Act.
comments:
NOTE: This Court resides in a
County owned building. The County
is responsible for ensuring
compliance and such records are
prepared by and maintained by
those County Officials.

series_description: Records of temporary usefulness that are not an integral part of a records series of the agency, that are not regularly filed within the agency’s recordkeeping system, and that are required only for a limited period of time for the completion of an action by an official or employee of the agency or in the preparation of an ongoing records series. Transitory records are not essential to the fulfillment of statutory obligations or to the documentation of agency functions. Some examples of transitory information, which can be in any medium (voice mail, fax, email, hard copy, etc.) are routine messages; telephone message notifications; internal meeting notices; routing slips; incoming letters or memoranda of transmittal that add nothing of substance to enclosures; and similar routine information used for communication, but not for the documentation, of a specific agency transaction.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Records management
officers should use caution in
assigning this records series item
number to records of the agency to
make certain they are not part of
another records series listed in this
schedule or, for records series
unique to the agency, are not part
of a records series that documents
the fulfillment of the statutory
obligations of the agency or the
documentation of its functions.
The disposal of transitory
information need not be
documented through destruction
signoffs (1.2.001) or in records
disposition logs (1.2.010), but
agencies should establish
procedures governing disposal of
these records as part of its records
management plan (1.2.014).

series_description: Minutes or notes, and supporting documentation taken at internal agency staff meetings.

Any records of the agency needed for the documentation of output, outcome, efficiency, and explanatory measures in the agency's appropriations request or strategic plan, and for performance measures used to manage the agency.
comments:
CAUTION: The FE + 3 retention
period overrides any shorter
retention period for a records
series in this schedule if the records
series is needed for documentation
of the entity’s performance
measures. NOTE: Monthly and
Annual Reports are generated
through Case Management and
subsequently forwarded to the
OCA, the LBB and/or the Office of
the Governor, Budget Division.

series_description: Quarterly and annual reports on agency performance measures submitted to executive and legislative offices.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
NOTE: This information is entered
electronically into the Automated
Budget Estimate System of Texas
(ABEST) system on an annual basis.

series_description: Reports compiled by court personnel on a daily or other periodic basis pertaining to workload monitoring, task completion, number of public contacts, and similar activities.
comments:
NOTE: Internal reports prepared
by/maintained by Clerks Office
Staff that are generated through
the Case Management System,
excluding those reports
appropriately addressed by item
number 1.1.064. CAUTION: SEE
item 1.1.064.

series_description: Manuals, guidelines, administrative rules, or similar records distributed internally for the use of employees or externally to the public or those individuals or entities regulated by an agency that sets out the rules, policies, and procedures that govern an agency's programs, services, or projects. Includes working files and related documentation used in creating rules, policies, and procedures.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Working files and
related documentation used in
creating the final rules, policies,
and procedures, are not subject to
archival requirement and may be
disposed of at the expiration of the
retention period.

series_description: Listings of all instruments filed and actions taken during the hearing and trial of a case.
comments:
Records may be maintained in an
electronic storage format. TX Gov’t
Code §51.205(a).

series_description: This series captures the formal, written explanations by a judge, or panel of judges, of how the court arrived at its rulings.
comments:
Records may be maintained in an
electronic storage format. TX Gov’t
Code §51.205(a). CAUTION: Does
not include legal opinions or advice
rendered on a case in litigation or
with regard to pending litigation.

series_description: Proceedings of the Court, except opinions and orders on motions.
comments:
Judgments & Orders-Agency of
origin retains permanent record
copy. The archival requirement will
be met by sending a copy to State
Archives. Records may be
maintained in an electronic storage
format. TX Gov’t Code §51.205(a).

series_title: Meeting Agenda and Minutes (of the Court)
series_description: Minutes or notes, and supporting documentation, and agenda of internal judges’ conference.
comments:
Records may be maintained in an
electronic storage format. TX Gov’t
Code §51.205(a).

series_description: Cases not successfully appealed to a higher Court.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
Records may be maintained in an
electronic storage format. TX Gov’t
Code §51.205(a).

series_description: Sentence of 20 years or less (Cases not successfully appealed to a higher court.)
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 25 years
comments:
Records may be maintained in an
electronic storage format. TX Gov’t
Code §51.205(a).

series_description: Sentence of more than 20 years
comments:
Records may be maintained in an
electronic storage format. TX Gov’t
Code §51.205(a).

series_description: Use of Original Exhibits by Appellate Court. If the trial court determines that original exhibits should be inspected by the appellate court or sent to that court in lieu of copies, the trial court must make an order for the safekeeping, transportation, and return of those exhibits. The order must list the exhibits and briefly describe them. To the extent practicable, all the exhibits must be arranged in their listed order and bound firmly together before being sent to the appellate clerk. On any party's motion or its own initiative, the appellate court may direct the trial court clerk to send it any original exhibit.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
After the mandate has been issued,
original exhibits in criminal cases
are returned to the trial court. In
civil cases, a letter is issued
informing the parties that the
original exhibit will be destroyed if
not retrieved within a set number
of days from the date of the letter.
At an earlier date if ordered by the
Court. Records may be maintained
in an electronic storage format. TX
Gov’t Code §51.205(a).
legal_citation:
Tex. Gov’t Code Ann.
§ 51.204(b)(2) and
Tex. Rules of App.
Proc. 34.6(g)(2).

series_description: A records retention schedule (i.e. form SLR 105) that identifies the records that are created and maintained by an agency, provides the minimum timeframes the records must be retained, and includes instructions for their disposition. This series may include working files and documentation of certification and approval by the Texas State Library and Archives Commission.
comments:
Original is retained for 50 years by
the State and Local Records
Management Division, Texas State
Library and Archives Commission.

series_description: Forms used to track the transmittal of records to/from onsite and offsite storage areas or a transfer of physical custody.

Logs or similar records listing records destroyed or transferred to the Archives and Information Services Division, Texas State Library and Archives Commission, showing records series title, dates of records, and date destroyed or transferred.
comments:
CAUTION: Disposition can mean
destroyed or transferred.

series_description: Worksheets or working papers used to capture records inventory information, including location tracking and to document existence and requirements of a records series.
Records management plans and similar records that establish the policies and procedures under which records and information are managed in the agency.

One copy of each state publication as defined on page xi of the State Records Retention Schedule, except a publication that is subject to a different retention period. For example, item numbers 1.1.004, 1.1.068, and 4.5.003, which also meet the definition, are closely associated with the appropriations process and must be retained AC + 6.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
CAUTION: Many state publications
must be submitted to the Texas
State Publications Depository
Program, Texas State Library and
Archives Commission, by law
(Government Code, 441.103-
441.105). The Texas State Library
and Archives Commission will
retain a copy of all state
publications submitted to it on a
continuing basis, subject to
periodic evaluation to determine if
the publication merits further
retention. For additional
information and requirements
concerning state publications made
available to the public through the
internet, consult the administrative
rules of the Texas State
Publications Depository Program of
the Texas State Library and
Archives Commission (13 TAC §§3.1-
3.8).

series_description: Agency-developed automated software applications, operating system files, and associated processing files, including job control language, programs, applications, scripts, source code, etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Software needed for
access to electronic records must
be retained for the period of time
required to access the records.

series_description: Records documenting operational and maintenance requirements of computer hardware such as operating manuals, hardware/operating system requirements, hardware configurations, and equipment control systems.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Hardware needed for
access to electronic records must
be retained for the period of time
required to access the records.

series_description: Records adequate to specify all technical characteristics necessary for reading or processing of electronic records and their timely, authorized disposition including documentation describing how a system operates and necessary for using the system such as user guides, system or sub- system definitions, system specifications, input and output specifications, and system flow charts; program descriptions and documentation such as program flowcharts, program maintenance logs, change notices, and other records that document modifications to computer programs; and data documentation necessary to access, retrieve, manipulate, and interpret data in an automated system such as a data element dictionary, file layout, code book or table, and other records that explain the meaning, purpose, structure, logical relationships, and origin of the data elements.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Hardware and software
needed for access to electronic
records must be retained for the
period of time required to access
the records.

series_description: Files needed for electronic data audits such as files or reports showing transactions accepted, rejected, suspended, and/or processed; history files/tapes; records of online updates to application files or security logs.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description: Manuals, guidelines, or similar documents establishing data processing policies and procedures in the agency in such areas as access and security, systems development, data retention and disposition, data ownership, production control, system back-up, etc.
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include
technical documentation of
procedures necessary for reading
or processing of electronic records.
SEE item number 2.1.009.

series_title: Software Registrations, Warranties and License Agreements
series_description: Records documenting the registration and licensing of a software application to activate the software for legal use by the end users of a state agency, along with warranties providing that the software will perform in accordance with functional specifications.

Applications, resumes, transcripts, letters of reference, and similar documents whose submission by candidates for vacant positions is required on the application form, by application procedures, or in the employment advertisement.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description: Notes, memoranda, or reports relating to the counseling of an employee for work- related, personal, or substance abuse problems. Usually maintained at the supervisorial level except in those agencies with counseling staff.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description: Agency copies of information relating to the selection by employees of life, health, vision, dental, disability, and other types of insurance or benefits offered by the State of Texas to its employees.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Documents that serve
as payroll deduction authorizations
must be maintained for the
retention period prescribed for
item number 3.2.001.

series_description: Internal or external announcements or advertisements of job openings, promotions, training programs, or opportunities for overtime.

Includes notes of interviews with candidates, questions asked of applicants, audio and videotapes of job interviews, driving record and previous injury checks, pre-employment physical examinations, and all other records that document the selection process.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include
criminal history checks.

series_description: Job evaluations, performance appraisals, or other similar documents used to evaluate the performance of an employee.
Corrective actions are those actions which do not affect pay, status, or tenure and are imposed to correct or improve an employee's job performance.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
CAUTION: If, during the retention
period of this records series, any
part of this series is used to
document and support personnel
disciplinary action under item
number 3.1.021, all documentation
from this series used to support
disciplinary action must be retained
for the the minimum retention
period described by item number
3.1.021.

series_description: Disciplinary actions are those actions which may affect pay, status, or tenure. They are imposed to discipline an employee whose conduct is harmful to the best interests of the state, the agency, or the employee work force, and for failure to improve performance or conduct following imposition of corrective action. May include cause for demotion, suspension, or dismissal, and the reasons for failure to give written notice of resignation. May also be used to document evidence of employee self- improvement efforts, as well as favorable and unfavorable communication.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
NOTE: Retained in the individual
employee’s personnel files.

series_description: Forms or similar records used to create or change information concerning the records of an employee including pay grade, position classification, employee number, evaluation date, and termination of employment.
Job descriptions, including all associated task or skill statements, for positions in the agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description: Certificates of completion, transcripts, test scores, or similar records documenting the training, testing, or continuing education achievements of an employee.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description: Federal reporting form (Form I-9).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description: Retention period applies if the agency replies to the sender of a resume that it will be kept on file should future job openings occur.
comments:
SEE item number 3.1.014 for
resumes, whether solicited or
unsolicited, that are used in any
way in the employment selection
process.

series_description: Summary of applicant qualifications, evaluation basis for selection or rejection, original applications, job assignments, promotions, separations, layoffs, terminations, compensation records, conditions of work, time records, hours of training provided (separate from labor), minority status, and gender of all selected and rejected applicants.
Awards, incentives, tenure, etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description: Employee or applicant requests for reasonable accommodation under the ADA (Americans with Disabilities Act).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description: Documentation used to start, modify, or stop all voluntary or required deductions from payroll, including garnishment or other court-ordered attachments.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description: Payroll records and registers documenting employee earnings, wages, and pay. This may include but is not limited to payroll input records, summary statements, payroll vouchers, payroll detail sheets, and payroll history.
Includes 1099, W2, FICA, and other tax records.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description: Used to make increases or decreases to employees' gross pay, FICA, retirement, or in the computation of taxes.
Employer's copy of "Employee's Withholding Exemption Certificate."
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description: Records defining the wage or salary rate for each position in the agency expressed in dollars, grades, or step numbers.
Records and documentation relating to unemployment compensation claims, including reimbursement of funds disbursed by Texas Workforce Commission (TWC).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description: Forms used to deposit employee's earnings into a specificed personal account.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description: Records documenting the amount of pension or deferred compensation earned by individual employees.
Affirmative action plans for both regular employees and apprenticeship programs.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description: Employee benefit plans such as pension; life, health, and disability insurance; deferred compensation; etc., including amendments.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_description: Reports providing statistical information on labor force.
comments:
NOTE: Workforce Summary
Document.

series_description: Minimum information needed to verify employment. Includes name, social security number, exact dates of employment, last known address, and most recent public access option form.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 75 years
comments: SEE item number 3.1.038.

series_description: Records relating to review and monitoring of job classifications within the agency.
Reports to the agency from TWC or its predecessor pertaining to employees.

series_title: Reimbursable Activities, Requests and Authorizations to Engage in
series_description: Requests and authorizations for travel; participation in educational programs, workshops, or college classes; or for other work-related activities for which the expenses of the employee are defrayed or reimbursed.
Any internally distributed manuals, guidelines, or similar records that define agency wide policies and procedures concerning the personnel of the agency.
Any documents detailing the procedural duties and responsibilities of agency positions on a position-by-position basis.

Any reports compiled by the agency on aspects of personnel staffing, including listings of all staff by program or name, staff hired during a month, detailed listings of employees within its organizational structure, position vacancies, analyses of turnover rates, and seasonality of employment, etc.
Aptitude, competency, or skills tests and checklists required of job applicants or of current personnel to qualify for promotion or transfer, including validation records.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
CAUTION: One copy of each
different test (different in terms of
either questions or administration
procedures) should be retained for
the period indicated.

series_description: Completed aptitude, competency, or skills test papers and checklists of job applicants or of current personnel taking a test to qualify for promotion or transfer.
Includes documentation used to complete EEO reports.

Used to create employee leave balances, to transfer leave balances when an employee transfers positions, to adjust carry-over balances on August 31, to correct errors on leave accumulation, and to close out leave accounts on separated employees.
Cumulative report is issued each pay cycle and provides employee leave status information for each position.

Records documenting individual employee's hours worked, including work schedules and documentation evidencing adherence to or deviation from normal hours for those employees working on fixed schedules.

series_title: Time Off and/or Sick Leave Requests
series_description: Requests and authorizations for vacation, compensatory, sick, Family and Medical Leave Act (FMLA), sick pool leave, and other types of authorized leave, and supporting documentation.
Requests submitted, approvals, number of hours transferred in and out, etc.

Claims; invoices; statements; copies of checks and purchase orders; expenditure authorizations; ledgers; encumbrance, purchase, general journal, expenditure, and special vouchers; and similar records that serve to document disbursements, including those documenting claims for and reimbursement to employees for travel and other expenses.

Bill copies or stubs, statements, billing registers, account cards, deposit warrants, cash receipts, credit card receipts, receipts books, cash transfers, daily cash reports, reconciliations, general journal vouchers, special vouchers, and similar records that serve to document money owed to or received by a state agency and its collection or receipt.
Internal and external periodic fiscal management reports and associated worksheets, including federal financial reports, salary reports, HUB reports, investment performance reports, etc.
Required by the General Appropriations Act. Includes encumbrances and documentation about budget transfers and revisions, as well as detail charts of accounts.
Periodic reports compiled from information entered into the Uniform Statewide Accounting System (USAS).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description: Yearly report compiled from information entered into the Uniform Statewide Accounting System (USAS).
Records documenting the internal and external procedural requirements with respect to the accounting department of a state agency.
comments: The State Comptroller’s Manual

series_description: Records of accounts deemed uncollectible, including write-off authorizations and returned checks, warrants, and drafts.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description: Documentation relating to the capital and fixed assets of a state agency, including equipment or property history cards containing data on initial cost, depreciation schedules or summaries used for capital outlay budgeting, and property sale, auction, or disposal records of agency owned equipment and property.
Records documenting requests for payment of a sum of money according to the terms of a policy or contract.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description: Bank statements, credit card statements, cancelled checks, check registers, deposit sllips, debit and credit notices, reconciliations, and other banking related records.
Signature cards or similar records establishing authority of the agency employee to initiate or authorize financial transactions on behalf of the agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description: Any mailing address, telephone or fax number, or e-mail address records maintained by the agency on its employees or on entities or persons it serves.
comments:
NOTE: Maintained in electronic
format in the court’s case
management system.

series_description: Records and reports of postage expenses, including postal meter usage.
Schedules of prices charged by the agency for services to the public or other agencies, including any documentation used to determine the charges.

Any internally distributed manual, guidelines, or similar records that establish standard office procedures for the agency; for example, agency style manuals, telephone protocols, mail room procedures, print shop and photocopy ordering instructions.
Any record created by the agency to track any type of incoming and outgoing correspondence or packages by the U.S. Postal Service or by private couriers.

series_title: Inspection, Repair, and Maintenance Records – Equipment
series_description: Equipment history file, which may include logbooks and requests for installation, moves, service, repair, etc.
comments:
For service agreements or
contracts related to equipment
repairs and service, retain in
accordance with item number
5.1.001.

retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_title: Estimate Files (Supply and Repair Cost Estimates)
series_description: Quotes for minor construction and repair projects and supplies. Includes supporting documentation, as applicable.
Requests or work orders for repairs or maintenance to facilities, vehicles, or equipment completed by internal agency personnel or external service providers.
Freight or cargo claims against carrier for damage or loss to a shipment.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description: Shipping information, including order acknowledgments, packing slips, and related documentation.
comments:
CAUTION: Some shipping
information may require longer
retention period. State agencies
must determine if longer retention
period is required based on the
type of materials shipped to or
from their agency.

series_description: Includes bid requisition/authorizations, invitations to bid or propose, bid specifications, accepted and rejected bids, and bid tabulation/evaluations. A) Associated with a contract executed, renewed, or amended on or after September 1, 2015.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years

series_description: Includes bid requisition/authorizations, invitations to bid or propose, bid specifications, accepted and rejected bids, and bid tabulation/evaluations. B) Associated with a contract executed, renewed, or amended on or before August 31, 2015.
Invalid bids that do not meet agency submission requirements and are not included in bid evaluation process (e.g. withdrawn, missed submission deadline, incomplete submission, etc.)
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description: Log, register, etc., providing a record of purchase orders issued, orders received, and similar data on procurement status.

Records relating to the issuance of keys, identification cards, building passes, passwords, signed statements or similar instruments of access to agency or state facilities, equipment or automated systems.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description: Plans developed (per Texas Labor Code, 412.054) to mitigate the damage of potential events that could endanger an agency's ability to function. Includes disaster preparedness, response, and recovery plans.
